question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile containing a transaction with a null TransactionRecord, causing recordItem.getTransactionRecord() to return null inside RecordItemAggregator.accept(), leading to a NullPointerException that aborts doParse() mid-stream, leaving the parserContext in a partially populated state that gets cleared by the finally block, causing all transactions processed before the null record to be rolled back?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.update()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with zero contract transactions, causing RecordItemAggregator.update() to call recordFile.setGasUsed(0L) and recordFile.setLogsBloom(LogsBloomFilter.EMPTY), overwriting any previously set gasUsed or logsBloom values on the RecordFile object, and if the RecordFile was pre-populated with correct values from a prior migration, causing those correct values to be replaced with zeros?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated transactionType integer value that maps to a valid TransactionType enum entry but does not match the actual transaction body type, causing the wrong TransactionHandler to be selected in EntityRecordItemListener.onItem(), leading to incorrect entity extraction and persistence of wrong entity associations in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parse(RecordFile)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the synchronized keyword on parse(RecordFile) by submitting a transaction that causes a very long-running parse (e.g., via a huge number of items or slow database writes), blocking all other threads from calling parse() and effectively creating a single-threaded bottleneck that denies service to all other record file processing?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where recordFile.getSidecars() returns a list containing a SidecarFile with a null or mismatched hash, causing parserContext.addAll(recordFile.getSidecars()) to add a corrupt sidecar to the context, which is then flushed to the database with incorrect hash values, allowing tampered sidecar data (contract state changes, bytecode) to be persisted as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the ContractFunctionResult bloom field contains all 0xFF bytes (all bits set), causing logsBloom.or() to set all bits in the block-level LogsBloomFilter, making the bloom filter useless (always returning true for couldContain()), and causing all subsequent log filter queries for that block to return false positives for every possible address and topic?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a v6 RecordFile with a blockNumber (index) of Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the offset calculation recordFile.getIndex() - lastRecordFile.getIndex() - 1 to overflow to Long.MAX_VALUE, and then repository.updateIndex(Long.MAX_VALUE) to add Long.MAX_VALUE to every block index in the database, causing all block indices to overflow and become negative or wrap around?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the items list contains the same RecordItem object multiple times (duplicate references), causing recordItemListener.onItem() to be called multiple times for the same transaction, leading to duplicate database inserts or constraint violations that abort the transaction and roll back all legitimate transactions in the file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated consensusTimestamp that is identical to an existing transaction in the database, causing a primary key constraint violation in recordItemListener.onItem(), which triggers a @Transactional rollback, and then the @Retryable mechanism retries the same file repeatedly, causing the importer to be stuck in an infinite retry loop for that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where ContractFunctionResult.getDefaultInstance().equals(result) returns false even though the result is semantically empty (e.g., only default field values set but with a non-default gasUsed of 0), causing the aggregator to add 0 to gasUsed and OR an empty bloom, incrementally corrupting the block-level aggregates with no-op operations that mask the true state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile with a version field set to exactly VERSION (6), while the previous file has version 5, causing updateIndex() to trigger the block number migration path, even if the file's actual content is tampered and the blockNumber field is set to an arbitrary value, allowing the attacker to arbitrarily shift all historical block indices?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: logItem()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with an extremely large memo or transaction body that when serialized via Utility.printProtoMessage() produces a multi-megabyte log line, causing log4j or the underlying logging framework to allocate excessive memory, leading to an OutOfMemoryError that is excluded from @Retryable and causes the importer process to crash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where recordFile.getConsensusEnd() returns a value that differs from the consensusTimestamp of the last item in the items list, causing applicationEventPublisher.publishEvent(new RecordFileParsedEvent(this, recordFile.getConsensusEnd())) to fire with an incorrect consensusEnd, misleading downstream event listeners about the actual time boundary of the parsed file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: parse(List)] Can an unprivileged external user submit a list of RecordFiles via parse(List<RecordFile>) where the list contains a null element, causing a NullPointerException in the for loop of AbstractStreamFileParser.parse(List), which triggers a rollback of all previously processed files in the batch, and then @Retryable retries the same null-containing list, causing repeated rollbacks and preventing any file in the batch from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered transactionFee in the TransactionRecord that is set to Long.MAX_VALUE, causing the chargedTxFee field stored in the database to be an astronomically large value, misleading users and applications querying the mirror node REST API about the actual fees charged for that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated receipt status code (e.g., setting result to SUCCESS when the transaction actually failed), causing recordItemListener.onItem() to process the transaction as successful, triggering insertTransactionSignatures() and processItemizedTransfers() code paths that are only meant for successful transactions, and persisting incorrect transfer and signature data to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a RecordFile where the items list is empty (zero transactions), causing recordFile.setCount(0L) and aggregator.update() to set gasUsed=0 and logsBloom=EMPTY on the RecordFile, and then parserContext.add(recordFile) to persist an empty block record, potentially overwriting a previously correct record for the same consensusEnd timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: updateIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a sequence of RecordFiles where the first is version 5 and the second is version 6 with a blockNumber equal to lastRecordFile.getIndex() + 1, causing the offset to be exactly 0 and updateIndex() to skip the repository.updateIndex() call, but if the actual v5 block numbers were already incorrect, the migration is silently skipped and the corruption persists?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a scheduleRef set in the TransactionRecord (making transaction.isScheduled() return true), causing onScheduledTransaction() to be called in EntityRecordItemListener, and if the corresponding scheduled transaction does not exist in the database, causing a foreign key constraint violation that rolls back the entire file's transaction and prevents all other transactions in the file from being persisted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a parentConsensusTimestamp set to a value that does not correspond to any existing transaction in the database, causing a foreign key or referential integrity violation when the child transaction is persisted via recordItemListener.onItem(), rolling back the entire file and denying service for all legitimate transactions in that file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: RecordItemAggregator.accept()] Can an unprivileged external user submit multiple contract transactions in a single RecordFile each with maximum gasUsed values, causing the gasUsed long accumulator to overflow through repeated addition, resulting in a negative gasUsed value being stored in the record_file table and causing incorrect gas usage reporting for that block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/record/RecordFileParser.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: doParse()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a tampered nodeAccountID in the transaction body that points to a non-existent node, causing EntityId.of(body.getNodeAccountID()) to return an invalid EntityId, which is then stored as the nodeAccountId of the transaction in the database, corrupting the node association data for that transaction?
